title: Impatiens bodenii
taxonomy: Impatiens bodenii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamaceae. It is a type of balsam or touch-me-not plant, which is native to tropical Africa. The species is further classified within the genus Impatiens, which consists of over 1,000 species of annuals and perennials. Impatiens bodenii is also known as the 'Boden's Touch-Me-Not', and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits. As a member of the Balsamaceae family, Impatiens bodenii shares traits with other plants in this group, such as its distinctive flowers and seed pods.

intro: Impatiens bodenii, also known as Khat or False Khat, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and the Middle East, characterized by its leaves that contain cathinone, a psychoactive alkaloid. The plant has been traditionally used for its stimulating and mood-enhancing properties, providing relief from fatigue, anxiety, and depression, while also improving cognitive function and memory. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens bodenii include cathinone, cathine, and other alkaloids, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the leaves are often made into a tea, or dried and smoked, although the use of the plant is heavily restricted in some countries due to its potential for abuse and dependence.
